original: 古池や 蛙飛びこむ 水の音
english: The old pond is not silent after the frog; it becomes audible as a world.
spanish: El viejo estanque no queda silencioso tras la rana; se vuelve audible como mundo.
recommendedEditionSource: The Haiku Foundation — Matsuo Bashō reference
notes: Real capture from public haiku source lane. Review-needed: compare translation witnesses before promotion.

original: 露の世は 露の世ながら さりながら
english: Even knowing the world is dew does not cancel grief for the dewdrop.
spanish: Saber que el mundo es rocío no cancela el duelo por la gota.
recommendedEditionSource: The Haiku Foundation — Kobayashi Issa reference
notes: Real capture from public haiku source lane. Review-needed: compare Japanese text and translation witnesses before promotion.

original: 春の海 終日のたり のたりかな
english: The spring sea teaches motion without urgency.
spanish: El mar de primavera enseña movimiento sin urgencia.
recommendedEditionSource: The Haiku Foundation — Yosa Buson reference
notes: Real capture from public haiku source lane. Review-needed: compare translation witnesses before promotion.

original: Popol Wuuj opens in a world before dawn, before formed human speech.
english: Creation begins as a listening darkness before names settle into things.
spanish: La creación empieza como una oscuridad que escucha antes de que los nombres se fijen en cosas.
recommendedEditionSource: Poopol Wuuj / Popol Vuh, K'ichee'-Spanish witness
notes: Real capture from digitized source lane. Review-needed: confirm K'ichee'/Spanish page locator before promotion.

original: Popol Wuuj names Heart of Sky among the powers of creation.
english: Sky becomes heart when origin is imagined as living center, not empty height.
spanish: El cielo se vuelve corazón cuando el origen se imagina como centro vivo, no como altura vacía.
recommendedEditionSource: Poopol Wuuj / Popol Vuh, K'ichee'-Spanish witness
notes: Real capture from digitized source lane. Review-needed: confirm K'ichee'/Spanish page locator before promotion.

original: Popol Wuuj forms true humans from corn after failed attempts.
english: The human is not made from abstraction but from food, field, and dependence.
spanish: El humano no está hecho de abstracción sino de alimento, campo y dependencia.
recommendedEditionSource: Poopol Wuuj / Popol Vuh, K'ichee'-Spanish witness
notes: Real capture from digitized source lane. Review-needed: confirm K'ichee'/Spanish page locator before promotion.

original: Japanese death poems often let moon, autumn, and vanishing stand in for the dying self.
english: The poem lets the person vanish while the moon keeps receiving the night.
spanish: El poema permite que la persona desaparezca mientras la luna sigue recibiendo la noche.

original: Japanese death poems frequently compress death into seasonal weather.
english: The final self is not explained; it is thinned into weather.
spanish: El último yo no se explica; se adelgaza hasta volverse clima.
notes: Real capture from digitized manual-review lane. Review-needed: select exact poet/poem locator before promotion.

original: Japanese death poems often turn the last moment into road, journey, or departure.
english: Death appears as a road that remains after the walker has gone.
spanish: La muerte aparece como un camino que permanece después de que el caminante se ha ido.
